query_summary: 01/01/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting the vehicle of security forces in Beir al-Abd, North Sinai, Egypt. Two security force members were killed and five others were injured in the blast. No group claimed responsibility for the incident.

query_record.summary: 01/01/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting the vehicle of security forces in Beir al-Abd, North Sinai, Egypt. Two security force members were killed and five others were injured in the blast. No group claimed responsibility for the incident.
query_record.weapdetail: A remote-controlled roadside bomb was used in the attack.
query_record.scite1: "Egyptian officials: Roadside bombing in Sinai kills 2 police," San Francisco Chronicle, January 1, 2021.

faiss_candidates.summary: 01/21/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting a military patrol vehicle in Sheikh Zuweid, North Sinai, Egypt. One soldier was killed and three others were injured in the blast. The Sinai Province of the Islamic State claimed responsibility for the incident.
faiss_candidates.gname: Sinai Province of the Islamic State
faiss_candidates.weapdetail: A remote-controlled roadside bomb was used in the attack.
faiss_candidates.addnotes: Casualty information conflicts across sources. Following GTD protocol, the majority reliable estimate is reported here.
faiss_candidates.scite1: "Egypt officials: Bomb kills 1 police and wounds 3 in Sinai," Lancaster Online, January 22, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite2: "Egypt: Bomb explosion kills at least two military personnel in Sheikh Zuweid, Jan. 21," GardaWorld, January 21, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite3: "New Sinai Attack Leaves Four Egyptian Security Officers Dead Or Injured," South Front, January 22, 2021.

faiss_candidates.summary: 02/20/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting a local market in Busayrah, Deir ez-Zor, Syria. At least three people were killed and five others were injured in the blast. No group claimed responsibility for the incident; however, sources attributed the attack to the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL).
faiss_candidates.gname: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL)
faiss_candidates.motive: The specific motive is unknown; however, sources noted that the attack came days after the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) announced the capture of 87 Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) militants in Deir al-Zour.
faiss_candidates.weapdetail: An explosives-laden motorcycle was used in the attack.
faiss_candidates.scite1: "Syria.- Three civilians killed by motorbike bomb explosion in northern Syria," CE Noticias Financieras English, February 20, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite2: "Casualties reported in eastern Syria town bombing," Middle East sources, February 20, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite3: "Three civilians martyred, ten others injured in motorcar bomb explosion in Deir Ezzor countryside," Syrian Arab News Agency (SANA), February 20, 2021.

faiss_candidates.summary: 01/21/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting an international coalition logistics supply convoy in Abu Ghraib, Al Anbar, Iraq. One security guard was injured in the attack. Ashab al-Kahf claimed responsibility for the incident.
faiss_candidates.corp1: Combined Joint Task Force – Operation Inherent Resolve (CJTF-OIR)
faiss_candidates.target1: Combined Joint Task Force Supply Convoy
faiss_candidates.weapdetail: A roadside bomb was used in the attack.
faiss_candidates.scite1: "Two Roadside Bomb Attacks Target US Convoys in Southern Iraq," Islam Times, January 22, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite2: "A military convoy of the international coalition targeted an explosive device west of Baghdad," Al-Sharqiya, January 21, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite3: "ISHM: JANUARY 14 - JANUARY 21, 2021," Enabling Peace in Iraq Center, January 21, 2021.

faiss_candidates.summary: 06/27/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting security forces responding to an earlier incident in Riyadh, Kirkuk, Iraq. This was one of two related attacks in the area on this date. At least one police officer was killed and at least two officers were injured across both attacks. No group claimed responsibility for the incident; however, sources attributed the attack to the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL).
faiss_candidates.gname: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL)
faiss_candidates.addnotes: Casualty numbers conflict across sources. Following GTD protocol, the majority reliable estimates are reported here. Casualty numbers represent a division of the total number of those killed and wounded across incidents 202106270018 and 202106270022. The victims included Abbas Karim and Hashim Kamil
faiss_candidates.scite1: "One killed and two injured in an ISIS attack on a site of the Federal Police in Kirkuk," Shafaaq.com, June 27, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite2: "ISIS launches an attack on a military point southwest of Kirkuk," Al-Sharqiya, June 27, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite3: "IS Militants Killed Two Iraqi Policemen in Kirkuk," BasNews.com, June 27, 2021.

faiss_candidates.summary: 03/25/2021: An explosive device detonated targeting a logistical support convoy for the international coalition near Yusufiyah, Babil, Iraq. There were no reported casualties in the blast. The International Resistance claimed responsibility for the incident.
faiss_candidates.corp1: Combined Joint Task Force – Operation Inherent Resolve (CJTF-OIR)
faiss_candidates.target1: Combined Joint Task Force Supply Convoy
faiss_candidates.motive: The specific motive is unknown; however, sources said that the incident was a response to the US assassinations of Abu Mahdi al-Muhandis and Qassim Soleimani.
faiss_candidates.weapdetail: A roadside bomb was used in the attack.
faiss_candidates.scite1: "IED explosion targets a convoy of the Coalition in Baghdad," Shafaaq.com, March 25, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite2: "Bomb targets US-led coalition convoy in Baghdad," Al-Sumaria TV website, March 25, 2021.
faiss_candidates.scite3: "ISHM: MARCH 18 - MARCH 25, 2021," Enabling Peace in Iraq Center, March 25, 2021.
